title: Subqueries, CTEs, Set Operations, and Relational Reasoning · Spiral Data Review
summary: Operations needs reusable query stages for backlog comparisons, missing relationships, and cohorts across time windows. Learners retrieve rules and procedures before seeing feedback. Planned artifact: Extend the evidence record for a composable backlog-analysis query pipeline after a delayed changed case. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Choose scalar, row, and table subqueries whose cardinality matches their surrounding expression.
Distinguish correlated subqueries and use EXISTS or NOT EXISTS for semijoin and antijoin questions.
Use CTEs to name query stages while distinguishing readability from materialization guarantees.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.sql:
-- sql-basics cumulative relational data portfolio.
-- Each increment declares grain, predicts cardinality or changed rows, runs in fresh browser SQLite,
-- reconciles results, tests changed data, and records PostgreSQL or standard-SQL boundaries.

SELECT sqlite_version() AS browser_sqlite_version;

steps.title: Separate evidence from confidence · Review Set Operations
steps.instruction:
review case — Compare each ticket with its team's effort and severity peers, retain teams with no resolved work, and rank ties deliberately. Build named query stages, one changed window of 4 rows, and a reconciliation total. Competency probe: Use UNION, UNION ALL, INTERSECT, and EXCEPT with compatible columns and deliberate duplicate semantics.
